description_long: This object is a weathered, vintage-style gas pump standing on a rectangular concrete base. The main body is a tall, rectangular column with a blue, rusted metal exterior, showing significant wear and corrosion. The front features a shattered glass display window above a faded red and white AMOCO logo, with the pump nozzle and hose attached to the side. The hose is black and rubbery, looping down to rest on the base, and the nozzle is red with a metallic finish. The pump lacks any digital displays or modern features, emphasizing its retro design. The overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to the elements for a long time, giving it a distressed, abandoned look.
description: A weathered, vintage blue AMOCO gas pump with a shattered glass display, rusted metal body, and a red nozzle attached to a black hose.
description_view_0: The left side of the gas pump, showing the blue, rusted metal surface and the concrete base, with no visible branding or nozzle.
description_view_1: The front view displays the shattered glass window, faded AMOCO logo, and the red nozzle with black hose hanging to the right.
description_view_2: The right side reveals the nozzle holster, red nozzle, and the hose looped down to the base, with partial view of the front branding.
description_view_3: The back side shows a mostly plain, rusted blue metal surface with the hose extending around the side, and no visible branding or display.
attribute_fuel_pumps: One vintage-style fuel pump is present, with a tall rectangular blue metal body, a single red nozzle attached to a black rubber hose, and a shattered glass display. The pump features a faded AMOCO logo on the front and shows significant rust and wear. No digital displays are visible.
attribute_station_signage: A single AMOCO logo is visible on the front of the pump, circular in shape with red and white coloring and bold black text. No other signage, safety notices, or directional signs are present.
